body_txt: One of the downsides of being competent is that sometimes even allies or neutral parties will sometimes fear you. While some of said parties might only try to kill someone who's an actual enemy to them, others might attempt to kill someone because of the simple possibility that someone could be a threat. Even if someone has been nothing but helpful, that won't stop some people from killing that person out of simple paranoia . The type of threat the person might represent can vary; someone who supplies some sort of valuable resources, such as weapons, information, or food, might be killed out of fear that they could supply one of their customers' enemies. If the potential threat is a powerful ally, they might be killed because the killer fears that said ally might turn on the killer. A powerful neutral party might be killed because their killer fears that they could become an enemy in the future or become an ally to their killer's enemies. This trope is almost always committed by villains, as murdering non-enemies, especially one's own allies, is a decidedly unheroic trait. In addition, like any act of evil, it could easily backfire on the villain. If the would-be victim survives, then the villain's fear will likely become a reality because of their own actions . Other times, the villain might end up making more enemies than they could've avoided since they have just proven that not being an enemy won't guarantee that someone is safe from being killed. In most cases, a villain's actions in this manner will prompt a formerly-neutral hero to become a decidedly-active hero now that the hero's safety is no longer a guarantee. When a hero does do this, it's usually at best considered a morally gray action. At worst, it's a sign that they are degrading morality-wise and might be a sign that they are Slowly Slipping Into Evil . Super-Trope to Precrime Arrest , when someone is arrested because of a crime they might do, Nice Job Breaking It, Herod , when a villain tries and fails to kill a child who will defeat them, Do Unto Others Before They Do Unto Us , and Shoot the Dangerous Minion , for when a villain kills a minion for being too powerful. Compare Too Powerful to Live . See also Improperly Paranoid . Compare Assassins Are Always Betrayed . Can overlap with You Have Outlived Your Usefulness if someone pulls this on an ally or Awakening the Sleeping Giant if someone pulls this on a neutral party. Closely related to With Great Power Comes Great Opposition . See also Resignations Not Accepted , for cases where the departure of a character leads the organization to shut said character down because He Knows Too Much . Can overlap with Ungrateful Bastard if the ally or neutral party has been nothing but helpful to the aggressor. Compare Rewarded as a Traitor Deserves in the scenarios where a turn coat is executed because their demonstrated capacity for treason compromises the security of anyone they work for.
